paper_id: Morales-Ramirez et al. - An Ontology of Online User Feedback in Software Engineering
input_limitations: The Markdown extraction contains mojibake for some characters and figures/tables are represented as [FIGURE], but the main prose sections were readable.

evidence_quotes.quote: But we believe that the understanding of the type of concepts instantiated as information contained in user feedback would be beneficial to define new methods for its better exploitation.
In our opinion, a definition of the concepts that pertain to the context of user feedback, which would enable a shared understanding of this context, is still missing.

Our ontology aims first at supporting the understanding of the collection and the analysis phases of the feedback cycle, by means of representing how the online user feedback is elaborated and analyzed in terms of speech acts.

goals.justification: The authors explicitly frame the ontology as filling a missing conceptualization and supporting understanding of the user feedback domain and feedback-cycle phases. This directly matches the Understanding goal of clarifying concepts and supporting shared domain understanding.

evidence_quotes.quote: The main problem at interest in the considered scenarios is the analysis of the user feedback for the purpose of identifying new features or requirements to be implemented in next software releases.

What is important to highlight is that so far the ontology support us manually to characterize the email's body from a linguistic and intentional point of view (i.e., the AnalysisTechnique -speech acts-), but it's up to the Receiver (i.e., a possible developer from the XWiki community) who has to reflect about all the set of speech acts and their intentions, and classify the FeedbackAnalyzed according to the FeedbackTypes we provide for the FeedbackClassification .

goals.justification: The ontology is used to analyze feedback and support classification decisions about bug reports, feature requests, and requirements. This guides decision making about what needs attention without itself being the implementation of the software change.

evidence_quotes.quote: With the help of the user feedback ontology, in the following we define this tool-supported analysis method, which is an instance of AnalysisTechnique .

With the part of the ontology corresponding to the extended communication ontology (see Fig. 5) we defined the needed resources to be used in a tool for supporting an automated identification of speech acts expressed in messages of stakeholders.
In this part we put into execution the tool using a real dataset of FeedbackDescriptions .

goals.justification: The ontology is described as helping define resources used in a tool-supported analysis method and the tool is executed on a real dataset. The link to implementation is present but somewhat indirect because the paper focuses on the ontology and scenarios rather than detailed tool construction.
